<blockquote>
'''''Figure:''' Sleep and learning timeline. Sleep blocks are marked in <span style="padding: 3px; color: #fff; background-color: blue;">blue</span>. Learning blocks in <span style="padding: 3px; color: #fff; background-color: red;">red</span>. Total learning time on individual days is displayed on the right. The currently selected sleep block turns <span style="padding: 3px; background-color: yellow;">yellow</span>. Its length is displayed at the bottom. A sleep block whose color bleeds from <span style="padding: 3px; color: #fff; background-color: blue;">blue</span> into <span style="padding: 3px; color: #fff; background-color: magenta;">pink</span> denotes interrupted sleep (e.g. with an alarm clock). A sleep block with the color changing from <span style="padding: 3px; color: #fff; background-color: #000;">black</span> to <span style="padding: 3px; color: #fff; background-color: blue;">blue</span> marks a delayed sleep episode (e.g. caused by watching a late tennis match).''
</blockquote>
